data.title: The Reactor Core Equalizer
data.statement:
The goal is to make all values in an [[N]] × [[N]] integer grid equal, using the minimum number of operations.

One operation chooses exactly one of the following:

Choose a row index [[i]] and increase every cell in row [[i]] by {{1}}
Choose a column index [[j]] and increase every cell in column [[j]] by {{1}}

Operations may be applied any number of times.
Values can only increase.

Compute the minimum total number of operations required to make all grid values equal.
If it is impossible, output {{-1}}.
data.stubGenerator:
read n:int
loop n loopline n cell:int
write answer
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1>>
An integer [[N]], the size of the grid.
<<Next [[N]] lines>>
Each line contains [[N]] space‑separated integers representing the grid values.
data.outputDescription: A single integer — the minimum number of operations required to stabilize the reactor grid, or {{-1}} if it is not possible.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

The goal is to make all values in an N × N integer grid equal, using the minimum number of operations.

One operation chooses exactly one of the following:

Choose a row index i and increase every cell in row i by 1
Choose a column index j and increase every cell in column j by 1

Operations may be applied any number of times.
Values can only increase.

Compute the minimum total number of operations required to make all grid values equal.
If it is impossible, output -1.
Input
Line 1
An integer N, the size of the grid.
Next N lines
Each line contains N space‑separated integers representing the grid values.
Output
A single integer — the minimum number of operations required to stabilize the reactor grid, or -1 if it is not possible.
Constraints
1 ≤ N ≤ 200
0 ≤ grid values ≤ 10^9
The result fits in a 64‑bit signed integer
Example
Input
2
4 1
0 3
Output
-1
